dialogueChunks.intro: The council heard the evidence. The powerful are furious, but they can't deny the numbers.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} brokered a new accord: fair compensation, community funds restored, and a public accounting system so nobody can skim again. Whether the apologies from the powerful were sincere is debatable. Whether it matters is less so.
dialogueChunks.outro: The settlement won't change overnight, {player_name}. Power doesn't redistribute quietly. But the workers have a seat at the table now. {quest_ally} says the first fair payroll goes out next week.

playerResponses.text: A seat at the table is where change starts. Good luck to them.
Keep watching the books. Corruption doesn't stay gone on its own.

dialogueChunks.intro: The founding document was clear. The levies were illegal. The guards opened the storehouse doors when they saw the proof.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The families got everything back. {target_npc} supervised the distribution personally. The one who imposed the false debts hasn't been seen since. {quest_ally} organized a watch to make sure it stays fair.
dialogueChunks.outro: A piece of paper with old words on it broke a tyrant's grip, {player_name}. That's the thing about legitimate authority: it only works as long as nobody reads the fine print. You read it.

playerResponses.text: The law was always on their side. They just needed someone to prove it.
The powerful will try again. Stay vigilant.
Next time, don't wait for an outsider. Fight for your own.

dialogueChunks.intro: The neutral authority ruled unanimously. The {quest_focus} {quest_focus_is} suspended pending a complete overhaul. Those responsible have been stripped of their positions.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The expelled worker was reinstated with a formal apology. {target_npc} says many others who were failed unfairly are being reassessed. {quest_ally} is drafting new standards with input from every level.
dialogueChunks.outro: The powerful won't forgive you easily, {player_name}. But the workers who can finally earn what they deserve will remember. Sometimes the underdog just needed a fair chance, not a revolution. You gave them one.

playerResponses.text: Fair chances shouldn't require someone breaking the system. Fix it properly.
The guilty got off easy. But at least the system changes.
